primary_sources: StatPearls NCBI (NBK539882, NBK519007, NBK470578, NBK545142, NBK553183, NBK534861)
notes: QIF band assignments are functional estimates based on the hourglass model.
All DSM-5-TR or clinical references are for threat modeling purposes, not diagnostic claims.
M1/M2 microglia polarization is flagged as contested and oversimplified; spectrum model preferred.

blood_brain_barrier.description: Tight junction integrity at I0 determines what signals and substances reach N1-N3 biology. Pericyte-mediated capillary tone regulation influences local signal fidelity.

components.name: Brain Capillary Endothelial Cells
components.description: Primary structural barrier. Extremely low transcytosis rate, lack of fenestrations, expression of tight junction complexes. Properties induced by contact with pericytes and astrocytic end-feet.

components.name: Pericytes
components.signaling: PDGFB/PDGFRb required for pericyte survival and BBB maintenance
components.pathology: Loss of pericytes associated with BBB breakdown in neurodegeneration (Alzheimer's, ALS)

components.name: Astrocytic End-feet
components.aqp4_localization: Highly polarized square arrays on vascular-adjacent end-feet
components.function: BBB induction via signaling to endothelial cells; structural basis of glymphatic system

tight_junction_proteins.name: Claudin-5
tight_junction_proteins.structure: Tetraspan; 2 extracellular loops, N+C cytoplasmic tails
tight_junction_proteins.function: Primary paracellular seal; PDZ motif binds ZO-1 PDZ1 domain
tight_junction_proteins.regulation: Phosphorylation at Y207 (Rho kinase) reduces TEER

tight_junction_proteins.name: Occludin
tight_junction_proteins.structure: MARVEL domain tetraspan; 2 ECLs; long C-terminal tail
tight_junction_proteins.function: Assembly and stabilization; forms higher-order oligomers
tight_junction_proteins.regulation: Redox-sensitive disulfide bonds on ECL2; phosphoserine/phosphothreonine supports integrity; phosphotyrosine disrupts it

tight_junction_proteins.name: ZO-1
tight_junction_proteins.structure: MAGUK family; 3 PDZ domains, SH3, GuK, proline-rich, actin-binding C-terminus
tight_junction_proteins.function: Scaffolding; tethers claudins (PDZ1) and occludin (SH3-GuK) to actin cytoskeleton
tight_junction_proteins.regulation: Phosphorylation at T770/772 modulates protein-protein interactions

tight_junction_proteins.name: ZO-2
tight_junction_proteins.function: Heterodimerizes with ZO-1 via PDZ2; redundant scaffolding

tight_junction_proteins.name: Tricellulin
tight_junction_proteins.function: Seals tricellular contacts where three cells meet

permeability.freely_crosses: Small lipophilic molecules (<400-500 Da), O2, CO2, ethanol, some anesthetics
permeability.does_not_cross: Most drugs (>98%), large proteins (antibodies), hydrophilic molecules, charged particles

blood_brain_barrier.bci_relevance: Implanted devices bypass BBB locally. FUS + microbubble cavitation transiently opens tight junctions by widening paracellular spaces and modulating claudin-5/occludin expression. Effect reversible within hours. Enables nanoparticle (3-15 nm optimal) and LNP-mRNA delivery. Proposed mechanism for both therapeutic and adversarial payload delivery to brain tissue.

venous_drainage.name: Superior sagittal sinus (SSS)
venous_drainage.function: Drains cortical surface veins; primary site of CSF reabsorption via arachnoid granulations

venous_drainage.name: Straight sinus
venous_drainage.function: Confluence of ISS + great cerebral vein (of Galen)

venous_drainage.name: Sigmoid sinuses
venous_drainage.function: Paired; S-shaped continuation to internal jugular vein (IJV)

venous_drainage.name: Cavernous sinuses
venous_drainage.function: Paired; surround sella turcica; contain CN III, IV, V1, V2, VI and ICA

cerebral_blood_supply.venous_outflow_path: Sigmoid sinuses -> IJV -> subclavian vein -> superior vena cava
cerebral_blood_supply.venous_note: No valves -- allows bidirectional flow with pressure changes

circle_of_willis.description: Pentagonal anastomotic ring at the base of the brain connecting anterior and posterior circulations
circle_of_willis.threat_note: High individual variation is important for threat modeling of targeted neurovascular attack

mechanisms.name: Myogenic (Bayliss effect)
mechanisms.mediator: Vascular smooth muscle contraction in response to wall tension

mechanisms.name: Metabolic/CO2
mechanisms.mediator: PaCO2: every 1 mmHg increase = ~4% CBF increase (vasodilatory via pH drop)

mechanisms.name: Nitric oxide (NO)
mechanisms.mediator: eNOS in endothelium; iNOS in astrocytes/microglia
mechanisms.notes: Vasodilation; also produced in neurovascular coupling

neurovascular_coupling.description: Mechanistic basis of fMRI BOLD and fNIRS signals. Neural activity drives local blood flow changes via molecular signals from the neurovascular unit.
mechanisms: Glutamate release from presynaptic neuron activates postsynaptic neurons and nearby astrocytes
Astrocyte metabotropic glutamate receptors (mGluRs) trigger IP3-mediated intracellular Ca2+ rise
Ca2+ activates phospholipase A2 (PLA2) -> arachidonic acid (AA) release
AA metabolized to PGE2 via COX-2, and to EET via CYP450
PGE2 and EETs act on smooth muscle cells -> vasodilation
Ca2+ also activates NOS -> NO -> vasodilation
K+ released from astrocytic end-feet acts on Kir channels on vascular smooth muscle -> vasodilation
Net result: increased CBF 3-5 seconds after neural activity onset (hemodynamic response function, HRF)

astrocyte_ca2_and_bold.evidence_level: Established at systems level (Petzold et al., Poskanzer et al.); precise quantitative relationship remains active research

neurovascular_coupling.bci_relevance: fMRI-BCIs and fNIRS-BCIs measure hemodynamic response, not direct electrical activity. NVC introduces 3-5s delay between neural event and detectable signal. Disrupted NVC (aging, TBI, neurodegeneration) degrades BCI signal quality. Adversarial relevance: pharmaceutical agents targeting NVC pathways (COX-2 inhibitors, NOS inhibitors) could in principle alter BOLD signal interpretation without altering underlying neural activity (proposed threat vector, not documented attack).

meninges.name: Dura mater
meninges.blood_supply: Middle meningeal artery (MMA) from maxillary artery via foramen spinosum
meninges.cell_subtypes_note: Five transcriptionally distinct fibroblast populations identified: dural border cells, arachnoid barrier cells, two inner arachnoid types, pial/perivascular fibroblasts
functions: Contains dural venous sinuses for venous drainage

meninges.name: Arachnoid mater
meninges.features: Avascular; bridges sulci without following their contours
meninges.granulations: Protrude into SSS; function as one-way ball-valves for CSF absorption into venous blood
functions: CSF reabsorption via arachnoid granulations into SSS

meninges.name: Pia mater
meninges.features: Follows brain surface contours exactly (all sulci and gyri); highly vascularized
meninges.perivascular_spaces: Forms Virchow-Robin spaces around penetrating vessels -- anatomical basis of glymphatic system

meningeal_spaces.name: Epidural
meningeal_spaces.clinical: Epidural hematoma from MMA rupture, temporal bone fracture; lens-shaped on CT

meningeal_spaces.name: Subdural
meningeal_spaces.clinical: Subdural hematoma from bridging vein rupture; crescentic on CT
meningeal_spaces.bci_relevance: Subdural ECoG electrode placement site; risk of subdural hematoma

meningeal_spaces.name: Subarachnoid
meningeal_spaces.clinical: Subarachnoid hemorrhage from Berry aneurysm rupture; communicates cisterns

cerebrospinal_fluid.production: Choroid plexus (modified ependymal cells + capillaries) in lateral, third, and fourth ventricles
cerebrospinal_fluid.production_mechanism: Active transport: Na+/K+-ATPase drives Na+ into ventricle, water follows osmotically; carbonic anhydrase contributes HCO3-
circulation_pathway: Foramen of Magendie (midline) + Foramina of Luschka (lateral)

absorption.classical: Arachnoid granulations/villi into SSS (pressure gradient 3-5 mmHg; one-way ball-valve mechanism)
contemporary_routes: Dural lymphatic vessels -> cervical lymph nodes
Perivascular lymphatics around basal cerebral arteries

composition.sodium: Higher than plasma (active transport by choroid plexus)

functions: Mechanical cushioning (buoyancy reduces effective brain weight from ~1400g to ~25g)

glymphatic_system.description: Brain waste clearance system active primarily during sleep
glymphatic_system.discovered: Nedergaard group, 2012-2013; published Science 2013 (Iliff et al.)
mechanism: CSF enters brain parenchyma via periarterial spaces (Virchow-Robin spaces), driven by arterial pulsatility
AQP4 on astrocytic end-feet (polarized to vascular surface) facilitates water movement from periarterial space into parenchyma
CSF mixes with interstitial fluid (ISF) in parenchyma
ISF + waste products drain via perivenous spaces
Perivenous outflow -> dural lymphatics -> cervical lymph nodes

aqp4_details.localization: Square arrays specifically on vascular-adjacent astrocyte end-feet (highly polarized)
aqp4_details.aging_change: AQP4 localization lost with aging (redistribution to broader astrocyte processes) -- major driver of age-related glymphatic decline

waste_cleared.amyloid_beta_note: 8.3% per hour in healthy young adults
waste_cleared.alzheimers_note: Age-related glymphatic failure is a proposed mechanism underlying Alzheimer's disease progression

glymphatic_system.bci_relevance: Chronic implants may disrupt glymphatic flow. Sleep deprivation-induced glymphatic disruption is a plausible threat vector for degrading long-term BCI signal quality. Glymphatic failure = accumulation of waste in neural parenchyma that could alter signal coherence metrics.

meningeal_lymphatics.discovered: Louveau et al., 2015, Nature (independently: Aspelund et al., 2015)
meningeal_lymphatics.structure: Conventional lymphatic vessels lined by LYVE-1+, PROX1+, PDPN+ lymphatic endothelial cells. Run parallel to dural venous sinuses and middle meningeal arteries.
functions: Drain immune cells (T cells, B cells, dendritic cells), small molecules, and excess fluid from CNS
Bridge CNS and peripheral immune system
CSF absorption (major outflow route, co-equal with arachnoid granulations in current models)
Tumor surveillance (meningeal lymphatic impairment associated with worse glioblastoma outcomes)
meningeal_lymphatics.aging: Meningeal lymphatics deteriorate with aging, contributing to impaired waste clearance and neuroinflammation -- parallels glymphatic decline
meningeal_lymphatics.bci_relevance: Meningeal lymphatics are a previously unknown route for CNS immune surveillance. Implant foreign body response (macrophage recruitment, T cell infiltration) may be modulated via meningeal lymphatic function. This is inferred, not directly demonstrated in electrode literature reviewed.

clinical_conditions.name: Meningitis
clinical_conditions.mechanism: Bacterial/viral invasion of subarachnoid space (S. pneumoniae, N. meningitidis)
clinical_conditions.bci_relevance: Contraindication to implant; marker in CSF (pleocytosis, elevated protein)

clinical_conditions.name: Hydrocephalus
clinical_conditions.mechanism: Impaired CSF flow or absorption; ventricular enlargement
clinical_conditions.bci_relevance: Can compress parenchyma; may preclude implant

clinical_conditions.name: ICP elevation (general)
clinical_conditions.mechanism: Mass effect, hemorrhage, cerebral edema, CSF obstruction

research_gaps: Quantitative model linking gliosis progression timeline to Cs (coherence metric) degradation: no published mapping exists between foreign body response histology and coherence metric parameters.
Specific gene expression signatures for all five meningeal fibroblast subtypes: established from single-cell sequencing (Louveau/Kipnis group, 2018) but not fully extracted.
Verified DOI for original Nedergaard glymphatic 2013 Science paper (10.1126/science.1241224) was not independently resolved via Crossref API in this session.
